name: Garlic Potato Puree W/Shitake Ragout and Potato Crisps
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons cornstarch dissolved in 1 tablespoon cold water
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the crisps: Brush a baking sheet with some oil. Slice one end of the potato diagonally and cut into diagonal slices. Arrange slices in a single layer on the sheet, brush remaining oil, sprinkle with salt, and bake at 400°F for 12-15 minutes or until golden brown. Transfer to a rack and cool.
2. Make the puree: Peel potatoes, quarter lengthwise, and cut into 1-inch pieces. Steam garlic cloves with potatoes (reserve 3 cloves) covered in steamer over boiling water for 12-15 minutes or until tender. Press through ricer, stir in butter, milk, salt, and pepper to taste, and keep warm.
3. Make the ragout: Heat oil in a large skillet over moderately high heat until hot but not smoking. Sauté mushrooms with reserved garlic cloves, minced, salt, and pepper for 5 minutes or until softened. Add broth and soy sauce, bring to boil. Stir in cornstarch mixture, stir into sauce, and simmer for 2 minutes. Stir in parsley.
4. Divide puree among 8 plates, mound it, arrange 3 crisps decoratively on each mound, spoon ragout over and around the puree.
